title: DSTAGNN: Dynamic Spatial-Temporal Aware Graph Neural Network for Traffic Flow Forecasting
key_contributions: Captures time-varying correlations at each time step
Significant improvements over static graph methods
abstract: Proposes dynamic ST-aware GNN with learnable graph structure at each time step. Captures both static topology and dynamic correlations. Strong performance but LargeST reveals scalability limitations on large graphs.
notes: ICML 2022. Strong performance on medium datasets. LargeST benchmark shows it cannot scale to GLA/CA due to memory requirements from dynamic graph generation.

title: Information Bottleneck-guided MLPs for Robust Spatial-temporal Forecasting
STD-MAE: Spatial-Temporal Dual Masked Autoencoder for Traffic Forecasting
abstract: Applies masked autoencoder paradigm to spatial-temporal traffic data. Pre-trains with dual masking strategy (masking both spatial nodes and temporal steps) to learn robust representations for downstream forecasting.
notes: IJCAI 2024. Brings self-supervised learning (MAE) to traffic forecasting. Growing trend toward pre-training approaches.

title: LSTTN: A Long-Short Term Transformer-based Spatio-temporal Neural Network for Traffic Flow Forecasting
key_contributions: Masked subseries Transformer to learn compressed contextual temporal representations from long historical series
Long-term trend extractor using stacked 1D dilated convolutions
Periodicity extractor using dynamic graph convolution for weekly and daily patterns
Short-term trend extractor using existing STGNNs for fine-grained predictions
Integrates long-term trends, periodic features, and short-term features comprehensively
abstract: LSTTN addresses limitations of existing STGNNs that only utilize short-range traffic data by comprehensively considering long- and short-term features. Uses masked subseries Transformer for efficient learning of compressed temporal representations from long series, extracts long-term trends and periodic features, then fuses with short-term features from STGNNs for accurate predictions.
notes: Published in Knowledge-Based Systems 2024 (293: 111637). Central South University and Xiangjiang Laboratory. Uses two-week historical data (4032 timesteps for 5-min intervals). Mask reconstruction with 75% random masking. Graph WaveNet adopted as short-term trend extractor. Demonstrates robustness to missing data and superior sensing of sudden traffic changes.

title: AutoSTF: Decoupled Neural Architecture Search for Cost-Effective Automated Spatio-Temporal Forecasting
How Different from the Past? Spatio-Temporal Time Series Forecasting with Self-Supervised Deviation Learning (ST-SSDL)
Efficient Traffic Prediction Through Spatio-Temporal Distillation
